abstract: Assess whether quantum reservoirs (with PCA-encoded inputs) can outperform classical ones and how entanglement changes feature mapping.

ai_review.improvements:
- Owner: revision. Regenerate Figure 4 with publication-ready legend strings (`Train-fold-only PCA`, `Intentional leakage test`) and verify `pdftotext paper/figures/fig_counterexample_stress.pdf - | rg -n "train_fold_only|intentional_leak_test"` returns no matches.
- Owner: writing_presentation. Add one explicit downstream equation reference sentence for `eq:q_feature`, `eq:gap`, and `eq:argmax` in method/results text; acceptance check: each equation label appears in at least one `\eqref{...}` or `\Eqref{...}` call outside its defining block.
- Owner: revision. Normalize auxiliary parity-audit table headers to publication labels (e.g., `Fold-local PCA only`) if the file is retained as supplementary material; acceptance check: no snake_case headers remain in `paper/appendix/appendix_hm_cf_1_parity_audit.tex`.
ai_review.label: omegaXiv AI Reviewer
ai_review.questions:
- Minor hygiene: Figure 4 legend in paper/figures/fig_counterexample_stress.pdf still exposes snake_case tokens (`train_fold_only`, `intentional_leak_test`) instead of publication labels.
- Minor equation cross-link polish: equations `eq:q_feature`, `eq:gap`, and `eq:argmax` are defined and used conceptually but not explicitly cross-referenced later with equation references.
- Minor auxiliary-artifact polish: paper/appendix/appendix_hm_cf_1_parity_audit.tex uses raw snake_case table headers (not a blocking manuscript issue because this file is not imported into paper/main.tex).
ai_review.strengths: Recovered writing_presentation output in validation-recovery mode, preserving the compiled manuscript package and fixing equation_usage_map compliance by providing non-empty equation_ref labels for all mapped equations.
ai_review.summary: The manuscript meets scientific adequacy for the stated hybrid quantum-reservoir objective: claims are aligned with user intent, theorem/lemma checks and symbolic guards are integrated with executed simulation evidence, and conclusions are appropriately conditional where evidence is mixed. Citation integrity, conference bibliography style, section depth, appendix substance, and page-budget requirements are satisfied. Remaining issues are publication-polish level (localized machine-style legend tokens and auxiliary artifact header normalization) and do not block approval.
ai_review.weaknesses:
- Minor hygiene: Figure 4 legend in paper/figures/fig_counterexample_stress.pdf still exposes snake_case tokens (`train_fold_only`, `intentional_leak_test`) instead of publication labels.
- Minor equation cross-link polish: equations `eq:q_feature`, `eq:gap`, and `eq:argmax` are defined and used conceptually but not explicitly cross-referenced later with equation references.
- Minor auxiliary-artifact polish: paper/appendix/appendix_hm_cf_1_parity_audit.tex uses raw snake_case table headers (not a blocking manuscript issue because this file is not imported into paper/main.tex).

artifacts_summary: Recovered writing_presentation output in validation-recovery mode, preserving the compiled manuscript package and fixing equation_usage_map compliance by providing non-empty equation_ref labels for all mapped equations.
evaluation_plan: Compare quantum and classical reservoirs under matched preprocessing (PCA), train/readout protocol, and reporting metrics (classification accuracy and error rates). Include ablation for entanglement on/off, observable-set choices, and dataset sensitivity (MNIST plus candidate alternatives if MNIST is too easy after PCA).
full_description: Investigate whether a quantum reservoir provides measurable advantage over classical reservoirs for image classification when inputs are PCA components encoded via angle encoding. Requested analysis includes the role of entanglement in the feature map, comparative behavior with and without entanglement, and use of a simple transverse Ising Hamiltonian reservoir with measurements of a subset of Pauli observables. The request flags MNIST as a possible baseline but questions whether PCA may already saturate separability, and asks whether alternative datasets would be better stress tests. Five reference PDFs were materialized under workspace/resources and should anchor notation and methodology for QELM/QRC, encoding expressivity, and kernel interpretations.
goals: Quantify whether PCA-encoded quantum reservoirs outperform matched classical reservoirs on image classification.
Isolate the contribution of entanglement by comparing entangling versus non-entangling reservoir dynamics under the same encoding and readout setup.
Characterize how reservoir dynamics transform input features, including observable-space representations derived from selected Pauli measurements.
Assess whether MNIST is sufficiently discriminative for this comparison and identify stronger alternative datasets if needed.

Include ablation for entanglement on/off, observable-set choices, and dataset sensitivity (MNIST plus candidate alternatives if MNIST is too easy after PCA).

original_problem.request_text:
Title: Quantum Reservoir Computing
Abstract: Assess whether quantum reservoirs (with PCA‑encoded inputs) can outperform classical ones and how entanglement changes feature mapping.
Full Description: I want to know if there is a quantum advantage in Quantum Reservoir Computing for image classification. If we take an image, and compute its PCA decomposition, and I feed the first X components to the reservoir as inputs (e.g. angle encoding), is there an advantage to using a quantum reservoir? What is the role of entanglement? How does the reservoir transform the features with and without entanglement? As a reservoir, we can take a simple transverse Ising model and angle encoding, and measuring a subset of pauli operators. You can try the MNIST dataset for digit classification, although I feel that the PCA is already so discriminatory (the only information in the picture is the digit, so *any* substantial difference between pictures immediately translates to a different classificaiton) that it will be hard to improve anything with QRC. Would other datasets be better test cases? 

Papers listing to download for review and context and notation:

https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.06873
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.00998
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.14677
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2008.08605
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2101.11020
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2407.02553
https://arxiv.org/pdf/2412.06758
Problem Type: Open Question
Difficulty: Advanced
Domain: Quantum Computing
Originator: Marius-Constantin Dinu
Compute Budget: 1 APU, Apple Silicon, 128GB
Required Accelerator: CPU only available
Target Deadline: 2026-03-10
Tags: quantum computing, reservoir computing
Categories: Quantum Computing
Expected Artifacts: Paper, Code, Package
Related Links: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.06873, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.00998, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.14677, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2008.08605, https://arxiv.org/pdf/2101.11020
Dataset Links: None

  sympy --> mpmath[mpmath]
package.install_command: pip install omegaxiv && ox install qrc-ising-core==0.1.0
